text:
Overview

This document defines recommended concurrency primitives, thread/actor affinity patterns, and safe integration points for Redwood's inference runtime and associated services. The goal is to reduce latency variability, avoid priority inversion on shared GPU/CPU resources, and make component-level behavior predictable under both steady-state and burst loads.

Audience
- Runtime engineers
- Infra and SRE teams
- SDK and client-library maintainers

Goals
- Provide low-friction, testable concurrency patterns that limit tail-latency impact on multi-tenant GPU hosts.
- Standardize affinity approaches across CPU-bound pre/post processing, scheduler threads, and GPU work submission paths.
- Give clear migration guidance away from ad-hoc locking and blocking I/O in hot paths.

Scope
This guideline covers:
- Inference serving processes (request dispatch, KV cache, batching)
- Pre/post-...
